code:
from dataclasses import dataclass

@dataclass
class Triple:
    subject: str
    predicate: str
    object: str

# EVOLVE-BLOCK-START

def predict(triples: list[Triple]) -> str:
    if not triples:
        return ""
    # Determine the primary subject (most frequent as subject)
    subject_counts = {}
    for t in triples:
        subject_counts[t.subject] = subject_counts.get(t.subject, 0) + 1
    main_subject = max(subject_counts, key=subject_counts.get)

    # Mapping of predicates to readable phrase templates
    # `{}` will be replaced by the object value
    phrase_map = {
        # Subject‑object oriented templates
        "cityServed": "{subject} serves the city of {object}",
        "elevationAboveTheSeaLevel": "{subject} is situated {object} metres above sea level",
        "elevationAboveTheSeaLevelInMetres": "{subject} is situated {object} metres above sea level",
        "elevationAboveTheSeaLevelInFeet": "{subject} is situated {object} feet above sea level",
        "location": "{subject} is located in {object}",
        "isPartOf": "{subject} is part of {object}",
        "country": "{subject} is located in {object}",
        "language": "{subject} main language spoken is {object}",
        "capital": "{subject} capital is {object}",
        "demonym": "{subject} people are called {object}",
        "operatingOrganisation": "{subject} is operated by {object}",
        "hubAirport": "{subject} has its hub airport at {object}",
        "headquarter": "{subject} has its headquarters in {object}",
        "runwayLength": "{subject} has a runway length of {object} metres",
        "runwayLengthFeet": "{subject} has a runway length of {object} feet",
        "runwayLengthMetre": "{subject} has a runway length of {object} metres",
        "runwayName": "{subject} has a runway named {object}",
        "runwaySurfaceType": "{subject} runway surface is {object}",
        # More natural runway surface phrasing
        "1stRunwaySurfaceType": "The first runway at {subject} is made of {object}",
        "2ndRunwaySurfaceType": "The second runway at {subject} is made of {object}",
        "3rdRunwaySurfaceType": "The third runway at {subject} is made of {object}",
        "4thRunwaySurfaceType": "The fourth runway at {subject} is made of {object}",
        "5thRunwaySurfaceType": "The fifth runway at {subject} is made of {object}",
        "1stRunwayLengthFeet": "{subject} first runway length is {object} feet",
        "2ndRunwayLengthFeet": "{subject} second runway length is {object} feet",
        "3rdRunwayLengthFeet": "{subject} third runway length is {object} feet",
        "4thRunwayLengthFeet": "{subject} fourth runway length is {object} feet",
        "5thRunwayLengthFeet": "{subject} fifth runway length is {object} feet",
        "1stRunwayLengthMetre": "{subject} first runway length is {object} metres",
        "2ndRunwayLengthMetre": "{subject} second runway length is {object} metres",
        "3rdRunwayLengthMetre": "{subject} third runway length is {object} metres",
        "4thRunwayLengthMetre": "{subject} fourth runway length is {object} metres",
        "5thRunwayLengthMetre": "{subject} fifth runway length is {object} metres",
        "1stRunwayNumber": "{subject} first runway number is {object}",
        "2ndRunwayNumber": "{subject} second runway number is {object}",
        "3rdRunwayNumber": "{subject} third runway number is {object}",
        "4thRunwayNumber": "{subject} fourth runway number is {object}",
        "5thRunwayNumber": "{subject} fifth runway number is {object}",
        "icaoLocationIdentifier": "{subject} ICAO code is {object}",
        "locationIdentifier": "{subject} IATA code is {object}",
        "iataLocationIdentifier": "{object} is the IATA location identifier for {subject}",
        "nativeName": "{subject} is also known as {object}",
        "owner": "{subject} is owned by {object}",
        "leader": "{subject} is led by {object}",
        "leaderParty": "The {object} is the lead party of {subject}",
        "leaderTitle": "{subject} has leader title {object}",
        "mayor": "{subject} has mayor {object}",
        "officialLanguage": "{subject} official language is {object}",
        "currency": "{subject} currency is {object}",
        "regionServed": "{subject} serves the region {object}",
        "class": "{subject} belongs to class {object}",
        "division": "{subject} belongs to division {object}",
        "order": "{subject} belongs to order {object}",
        "jurisdiction": "{subject} has jurisdiction over {object}",
        "countySeat": "{subject} county seat is {object}",
        "chief": "{subject} chief is {object}",
        "foundedBy": "{subject} founded by {object}",
        "foundingYear": "{subject} founded in {object}",
        "postalCode": "{subject} postal code {object}",
        "areaCode": "{subject} area code {object}",
        "ceremonialCounty": "{subject} ceremonial county {object}",
    }

    # Helper to format numbers with commas and strip unnecessary decimals
    def _format_number(val: str) -> str:
        try:
            # Try to interpret as float/int
            num = float(val)
            if num.is_integer():
                return f"{int(num):,}"
            else:
                return f"{num:,}"
        except ValueError:
            return val

    # Helper to format a single triple using the subject‑aware templates
    def format_triple(t: Triple) -> str:
        tmpl = phrase_map.get(t.predicate)
        if tmpl:
            # Strip surrounding quotes from the object if present
            obj_clean = t.object.strip('"')
            obj_formatted = _format_number(obj_clean)
            return tmpl.format(subject=t.subject, object=obj_formatted)
        # fallback: make predicate readable
        readable = t.predicate.replace('_', ' ')
        return f"{readable} {t.object}"

    # Separate triples about the main subject from the rest
    main_clauses = []
    secondary_clauses = []
    for t in triples:
        clause = format_triple(t)
        if t.subject == main_subject:
            main_clauses.append(clause)
        else:
            # Build a subordinate clause linking the secondary subject to its object(s)
            # e.g., "Tirstrup, which is part of the Central Denmark Region, is in Denmark"
            secondary = f"{t.subject}, which {clause}"
            secondary_clauses.append(secondary)

    # Combine clauses
    all_clauses = main_clauses + secondary_clauses
    if not all_clauses:
        return f"{main_subject}."
    # Build a fluent sentence
    sentence = f"{main_subject} " + ", ".join(all_clauses) + "."
    # Clean up possible double spaces
    return " ".join(sentence.split())

# EVOLVE-BLOCK-END
